article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall put in place a policy to comply with Union law on copyright and related rights, and in particular to identify and comply with a reservation of rights expressed pursuant to Article 4(3) of Directive (EU) 2019/790.
scoring_text: Knowledge of copyright compliance for AI model training, including policy development for Union copyright law, rights reservation identification and compliance, and text and data mining exception management under Directive 2019/790.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of copyright compliance for AI model training, including policy development for Union copyright law, rights reservation identification and compliance, and text and data mining exception management under Directive 2019/790.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of the regulatory and compliance landscape governing enterprise data and AI, including data protection law, AI-specific regulation, sector-specific obligations, and cross-jurisdictional compliance requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 53(1)(c) (obligation) superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.561, cosine: 0.630). Secondary: AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.628), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.617).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.561). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, data, knowledge, law]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [2019, 790, copyright, development, directive, exception] vs WIDAI terms [cross, enterprise, governing, jurisdictional, landscape, obligations] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.860, R=0.877). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (95.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.630 > STS 0.561 (gap +0.070). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, data, knowledge, law] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, data, knowledge, law]. EU AI Act-only: [2019, 790, copyright, development, directive, exception]. WIDAI-only: [cross, enterprise, governing, jurisdictional, landscape, obligations].
